name: Freerouting v2.2.4
body:
> If you would like to support the project, please consider [sponsoring me](https://github.com/sponsors/andrasfuchs). Even a small monthly donation is greatly appreciated!
> 
> You can also fuel my passion by sharing your success stories with Freerouting - send them to info@freerouting.app, I would love to read every one of them!

This release focuses on autorouter stability, fixing several edge-case failures—including infinite loops, crashes, and copper pour connectivity issues—that occurred on complex PCB designs. Key improvements include a new board-hash stagnation check, graceful handling of deep trace normalizations, and corrected layer initialization for DSN-based routing.

### Bug Fixes & Autorouter Stability

This release focuses on robustness improvements to the autorouter, fixing several edge-case failures that could cause the router to loop indefinitely, crash, or produce incorrect scores — especially on boards with copper pours (power planes) and complex net topologies.

- **Fix layer initialisation for DSN autoroute (Issue #676)**
  Corrects a layer-setup bug in the DSN autoroute code path that could produce incorrect routing on multi-layer boards.

- **Fix `max_connections` calculation for nets**
  The number of required connections per net is now computed correctly, preventing premature routing-complete detection.

- **Normalize trace lengths to millimetres for scoring**
  Trace-length scores were previously in internal units. They are now normalised to millimetres to make the score comparable across different board scales.

- **Improve autorouter stagnation and plane-net handling**
  The autorouter now correctly identifies when no progress can be made and exits cleanly instead of running indefinitely. Plane-net items that are already connected are skipped to avoid false-work and spurious "normalisation failed" warnings.

- **Stop autorouter on repeated identical board state**
  A board-hash stagnation check (matching v1.9 behaviour) is now applied between passes. When the same board state is seen twice, the router stops and reports the result rather than looping.

- **Avoid throwing on max normalisation depth**
  `PolylineTrace.normalize()` previously threw an exception when the maximum recursion depth was reached; this was silently swallowed and logged as a "normalisation failed" warning. It now returns `false` gracefully so the outer loop continues processing remaining traces.

- **Guard divide-by-zero in `getNormalizedScore()`**
  When a board is fully routed (`maximumCount == 0`), the score denominator is zero. The guard now returns `0f` in this case, preventing `NaN` comparisons that caused the stagnation check to malfunction.

- **Check search-tree entries before polyline join**
  `PolylineTrace.combine_at_end()` now verifies that both traces have entries in the default search tree before attempting the fast merge path, preventing a `NullPointerException` in `ShapeSearchTree.merge_entries_at_end`.

- **Restore best board; deduplicate `getImpactedPoints`**
  The best-known board state is now restored at the end of a routing run when the final pass is worse. Duplicate points in `getImpactedPoints` are removed to reduce log noise.

### Improvements

- **Log settings merge at debug level**
  Settings-merge operations are now logged at `DEBUG` rather than a higher level, reducing log noise in normal operation.

- **Update benchmark messages; remove unused import**
  Minor housekeeping in the benchmarking output and code.

### Documentation

- **Clarify routing algorithm explanation in architecture docs** (contributed by @26oppy)
  The routing algorithm section in `docs/architecture.md` has been updated with a clearer explanation of the wave-expansion and A*-based approaches.

- **Document autorouter plane & loop fixes**
  `docs/issues/Issue152-copper-pour-plane-awareness.md` and `AGENTS.md` updated to reflect the fixes applied in this release.

- **Fix image link format in `developer.md`**
  Corrected a broken Markdown image link.

- **Clarify Maven Central publishing step**
  The developer documentation for publishing to Maven Central has been updated for clarity.

### Infrastructure & CI

- **Fix `ValueError: too many values to unpack` when no JRE 25 is found**
  The Python helper script that detects a local JRE 25 installation now handles the case where no matching JRE is present without raising an unhandled exception.

- **Use `NetHttpTransport` and handle SES write errors**
  HTTP transport in the Google API client is switched to `NetHttpTransport` for compatibility. SES file write errors are now caught and logged rather than silently failing.

- **Add Gemini auth checks; adjust timeouts/filters**
  The Gemini CI workflows now validate authentication before executing, with adjusted timeouts and label filters for more reliable triage runs.

- **Increase `maxSessionTurns` and tighten triage prompts**
  Gemini CLI session configuration updated for more complete automated issue triage.

- **Quote env expressions with double quotes in workflows**
  GitHub Actions workflow YAML updated to correctly quote environment variable expressions, preventing potential shell-expansion issues.

- **Update KiCad Freerouting plugin to 2.2.4**
  The bundled KiCad plugin integration under `integrations/` has been updated to reference the 2.2.4 release.

### Refactoring & Build

- **Refactor publishing setup; Javadoc/signing fixes**
  Gradle publishing configuration refactored for cleaner Maven Central artifact generation. Javadoc task and artifact signing are now correctly wired.

### New Test Coverage

- **`Issue676RoutingTest`** — Full-pass routing test for the DSN autoroute layer-initialisation fix, ensuring the reported regression does not recur.
